title: Brik Project Configuration
description: Declarative project configuration for the Brik portable CI/CD pipeline system. Defines what happens at each stage (stack, tools, thresholds) without describing the pipeline structure itself. The fixed flow Init -> Release -> Build -> Quality/Security -> Test -> Package -> Deploy -> Notify is implemented by platform-specific shared libraries.

version.description: Schema version for brik.yml. Must be 1.

project.description: Project identity and metadata. Required. Provides the name, optional stack hint, and optional monorepo root.

name.description: Project name. Used in logs, notifications, and artefact labels.

stack.description: Technology stack. Optional - Brik performs auto-detection from project files (package.json -> node, pom.xml -> java, etc.) when omitted.

root.description: Relative path to the service root directory. Used in monorepos where each service has its own brik.yml.

build.description: Build stage configuration. Absent means use stack defaults (e.g. npm run build for node, mvn package for java).

test.description: Test stage configuration. Absent means use stack defaults.

quality.description: Quality stage configuration covering lint, formatting, and static analysis. Enabled by default.

security.description: Security stage configuration covering dependency scanning, secret scanning, and container scanning. Enabled by default.

package.description: Package stage configuration for building and publishing artefacts such as Docker images.

deploy.description: Deploy stage configuration. Defines one or more named environments with conditions and deployment targets.

notify.description: Notify stage configuration for Slack, email, and webhook notifications.

hooks.description: Pre and post stage hook scripts. Each hook receives the pipeline context and runs as a shell command.

command.description: Build command to execute. Overrides the stack default.

node_version.description: Node.js version to use (e.g. "20", "18"). Applicable when stack is node.

java_version.description: Java version to use (e.g. "21", "17"). Applicable when stack is java.

python_version.description: Python version to use (e.g. "3.12", "3.11"). Applicable when stack is python.

dotnet_version.description: .NET version to use (e.g. "8.0", "9.0"). Applicable when stack is dotnet.

rust_version.description: Rust version to use (e.g. "1.76", "stable"). Applicable when stack is rust.

framework.description: Test framework to use. Overrides the stack default (e.g. jest for node, junit for java, pytest for python).

coverage_threshold.description: Minimum code coverage percentage required to pass the test stage. Default is 0 (no minimum enforced).

commands.description: Explicit test commands per test type. When present, these override the framework-derived defaults.

enabled.description: Whether the quality stage is active. Set to false to skip lint, format, and SAST checks entirely. Default is true.

tool.description: Lint tool to use (e.g. eslint, checkstyle, ruff, golangci-lint, clippy). Overrides the stack default.

config.description: Path to the lint configuration file (e.g. .eslintrc.yml).

fix.description: Whether to run the linter in auto-fix mode. Default is false.

tool.description: Formatter to use (e.g. prettier, google-java-format, ruff format, gofmt, rustfmt). Overrides the stack default.

check.description: Whether to run the formatter in check mode (fail if files would be reformatted) rather than applying changes. Default is false.

tool.description: SAST tool to use (e.g. semgrep, sonarqube, codeql).

ruleset.description: Ruleset or profile for the SAST tool (e.g. auto, p/security-audit).

enabled.description: Whether the security stage is active. Set to false to skip all security scans. Default is true.

dependency_scan.description: Whether to scan project dependencies for known vulnerabilities.

secret_scan.description: Whether to scan the codebase for accidentally committed secrets, tokens, or credentials.

container_scan.description: Whether to scan the built Docker image for OS and application vulnerabilities. Typically meaningful only when package.docker is configured.

severity_threshold.description: Minimum vulnerability severity that causes the security stage to fail. Vulnerabilities below this level are reported but do not block the pipeline.

docker.description: Docker image build and push configuration.

image.description: Full image name including registry and repository (e.g. registry.example.com/my-service).

dockerfile.description: Path to the Dockerfile relative to the build context. Default is Dockerfile.

context.description: Docker build context path. Default is . (current directory).

platforms.description: Target platforms for multi-arch builds (e.g. linux/amd64, linux/arm64).

build_args.description: Docker build arguments passed as --build-arg. Keys and values must be strings.

environments.description: Named deployment environments. Each key is an environment name (e.g. staging, production). Each value defines the deployment condition and target.

when.description: Condition expression evaluated by the shared library to decide whether this environment is deployed. Supports branch equality (branch == 'main'), glob matching (tag =~ 'v*'), and native CI variables ($CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == 'merge_request_event').

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace for k8s and helm targets.

manifest.description: Path to the Kubernetes manifest file or directory for the k8s target.

repo.description: GitOps infrastructure repository reference (e.g. org/repo-name) for the gitops target.

path.description: Path within the GitOps repository where manifests for this service are located.

controller.description: GitOps controller responsible for reconciliation (e.g. argocd, fluxcd).

app_name.description: Application name as known to the GitOps controller.

notifyEventList.description: Pipeline events that trigger a notification.

channel.description: Slack channel name including the # prefix (e.g. #deployments).

on.description: Pipeline events that trigger a Slack notification.

to.description: Recipient email address or comma-separated list of addresses.

on.description: Pipeline events that trigger an email notification.
Pipeline events that trigger a webhook call.

hookCommandList.description: Ordered list of shell commands to execute at a hook point.

pre_init.description: Commands executed before the init stage.

post_init.description: Commands executed after the init stage completes successfully.

pre_release.description: Commands executed before the release stage.

post_release.description: Commands executed after the release stage completes successfully.

pre_build.description: Commands executed before the build stage.

post_build.description: Commands executed after the build stage completes successfully.

pre_quality.description: Commands executed before the quality stage.

post_quality.description: Commands executed after the quality stage completes successfully.

pre_security.description: Commands executed before the security stage.

post_security.description: Commands executed after the security stage completes successfully.

pre_test.description: Commands executed before the test stage.

post_test.description: Commands executed after the test stage completes successfully.

pre_package.description: Commands executed before the package stage.

post_package.description: Commands executed after the package stage completes successfully.

pre_deploy.description: Commands executed before the deploy stage.

post_deploy.description: Commands executed after the deploy stage completes successfully.

pre_notify.description: Commands executed before the notify stage.

post_notify.description: Commands executed after the notify stage completes successfully.
